en.question: A shopkeeper sells an article at a loss of 12.5%. If he had sold it for Rs 51.80 more, he would have gained 6%. What is the cost price?
en.explanation: Difference in selling prices = (6% + 12.5%) of CP = 18.5% of CP = Rs 51.80. So CP = 51.80 / 0.185 = 280.

hi.question: 12.5% हानि पर बेचता है। ₹51.80 अधिक में 6% लाभ होता। क्रय मूल्य?

en.question: A person bought 20 dozen eggs at Rs 30 per dozen. 20 eggs were broken. He sold the remaining eggs at Rs 3.50 per egg. What is his profit percentage?
en.explanation: CP = 20 x 30 = Rs 600. Total eggs = 240, remaining = 220. SP = 220 x 3.50 = Rs 770. Profit = 170. Profit% = (170/600) x 100 = 28.33%.

en.question: A dishonest shopkeeper uses a weight of 900g instead of 1kg and sells at cost price. What is his profit percentage?

hi.question: 1kg के बदले 900g तोलकर क्रय मूल्य पर बेचता है। लाभ%?

en.question: A shopkeeper sells an item at a loss of 12.5%. If he had sold it for Rs 56 more, he would have gained 12.5%. Find the cost price.
en.explanation: Difference = 25% of CP = Rs 56. So CP = 56 x 100/25 = Rs 224.

en.question: By selling 33 meters of cloth, a man gains the selling price of 11 meters. Find the gain percentage.

hi.question: 33m बेचने पर 11m के SP जितना लाभ। लाभ%?

en.question: A trader marked his goods 30% above the cost price. He allows a discount of 15% on the marked price. Find his profit percentage.
A person sells two articles at the same price. On one he gains 25% and on the other he loses 25%. What is his overall profit or loss percentage?

hi.question: समान मूल्य पर दो वस्तुएँ, एक पर 25% लाभ, दूसरी पर 25% हानि। कुल?

en.question: The cost price of 20 articles is equal to the selling price of 15 articles. Find the profit percentage.
